0.q: A pivotal regulatory system of the nitrogen-fixing soybean endosymbiont Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens for efficient symbiosis is the general stress response (GSR), which relies on the alternative sigma factor σEcfG.

1.q: the T6P pathway of trehalose biosynthesis, which uses T6P synthase (TPS or OtsA) to condense UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate to T6P.

2.q: T6P phosphatase (TPP or OtsB) catalyzes the conversion of T6P to trehalose and inorganic phosphate.

3.q: biosynthesis of trehalose is under GSR control, and mutants lacking the respective biosynthetic genes otsA and/or otsB phenocopy GSR-deficient mutants under symbiotic and selected free-living stress conditions.

4.q: biosynthesis of trehalose is under GSR control, and mutants lacking the respective biosynthetic genes otsA and/or otsB phenocopy GSR-deficient mutants under symbiotic and selected free-living stress conditions.
